title: quassia laevis
intro: Quassia laevis is a species of plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the yellow quassia or silky quassia, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a natural insecticide and anthelmintic, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Quassia laevis include its potential use in treating digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to aid in the treatment of parasitic infections. Herbal preparations of Quassia laevis can be made into a tincture, tea, or infusion, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
